g.text: Model-View-Controller Architecture in Java Web Applications

g.data-narration: MVC separates an application into three interconnected components: Model for data and logic, View for presentation, and Controller for input handling.
g.text: Model-View-Controller separates concerns for maintainable applications

g.data-narration: MVC enables code reusability, maintainability, and allows multiple views of the same data, essential for enterprise Java development.
The Model manages application data and business logic. In Java, it includes JavaBeans, DAOs, and various Model interfaces.
g.text: Manages data and business logic • Independent of UI

g.data-narration: The View renders the user interface. In Java web apps, JSP pages display data from the Model to users.
g.text: Renders UI • Displays Model data • No business logic

g.data-narration: The Controller handles user input, processes requests, and coordinates between Model and View using Servlets.
g.text: Handles HTTP requests • Invokes Model • Forwards to View

g.data-narration: The complete MVC flow: User sends request to Controller, Controller invokes Model, Model returns data, Controller forwards to View.
Swing provides specific Model interfaces: DefaultButtonModel for buttons, ListModel for lists, TableModel for tables.
When Model data changes, it fires events. Views listen to these events and automatically update their display.
g.text: Model fires events → View listens → UI updates automatically

g.data-narration: Swing uses a modified MVC where View and Controller are often combined into a single UI component for simplicity.
g.text: View + Controller combined into UI components

g.data-narration: Same model can drive multiple views. A ListModel can power both a dropdown and a list display simultaneously.
g.text: Data reusability: same model powers different views

g.data-narration: MVC provides easier testing, team collaboration, parallel development, and is the foundation for frameworks like Spring MVC.
g.text: Powers Spring MVC, Struts, and modern Java frameworks

g.data-narration: Remember: Model stores data, View displays it, Controller handles input. This separation enables maintainable, testable applications.
MVC is the foundation for enterprise Java development. Master this pattern to build scalable, maintainable web applications.

text.text: MVC: The Foundation of Enterprise Java Development
Master MVC to build scalable, maintainable web applications → Spring MVC
